name: Peanut Butter and Chocolate Cookie Sandwich Cookies
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Butter or margarine, softened and divided
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat oven to 350°F.
2. In a small saucepan over very low heat, melt peanut butter chips and 3 tablespoons softened butter. Remove from heat; cool slightly.
3. In a large mixing bowl, beat the remaining 1/2 cup softened butter and 1 cup sugar until light and fluffy. Add corn syrup, egg, and vanilla; blend thoroughly.
4. Combine 2 cups flour, baking soda, and salt in a separate bowl. Add to the butter mixture, blending well.
5. Remove 1 1/4 cups batter and place in a small bowl. Stir in the remaining 2 tablespoons flour and peanut butter chip mixture with a wooden spoon.
6. Blend cocoa, the remaining 1/4 cup sugar, and melted butter into the remaining batter.
7. Refrigerate both batters for 5-10 minutes or until firm enough to handle.
8. Roll both doughs into 1-inch balls; roll in additional sugar. Place on an ungreased cookie sheet.
9. Bake for 10-11 minutes or until set. Cool slightly, then remove from the cookie sheet to a wire rack. Cool completely.
10. Place 1 marshmallow on the flat side of 1 chocolate cookie. Microwave at MEDIUM (50%) for 10 seconds or until the marshmallow is softened. Place a peanut butter cookie over the marshmallow, pressing down slightly. Repeat with remaining marshmallows and cookies.
11. Makes about 2 dozen sandwich cookies.
